select_when: A fast, intuitive judgment has been made in a high-stakes or novel context and needs to be examined for reliability.
Cognitive biases driven by automatic pattern-matching (anchoring, affect heuristic, halo effect) are suspected in a decision.
Expert intuition is being applied outside the domain where reliable patterns were built, creating overreach risk.
A confident conclusion has been reached quickly and the fluency of the answer may be substituting for the rigor of the analysis.
avoid_when: Expert intuition in a well-practiced domain with fast, reliable feedback — in those conditions System 1 is accurate and deliberative override degrades rather than improves performance.
The model is being used to dismiss all fast judgment as unreliable without distinguishing calibrated expert pattern recognition from domain-overreached heuristics.
input_type: fast intuitive judgment or pattern-matched conclusion
output_type: bias identification and System 2 scrutiny trigger

curation_notes.summary: System 1 curated as a detection and scrutiny-trigger model — its activation surface is about recognizing when automatic processing needs deliberate audit, not about suppressing intuition categorically.
open_questions: The boundary between reliable expert System 1 and overreached System 1 requires domain context to adjudicate — the model's select_when conditions are structural rather than domain-specific.

select_when.note: Source markdown explicitly describes WYSIATI, halo, affect heuristic, and base-rate neglect as System 1 failure modes in high-stakes contexts — select_when conditions are grounded in these.

avoid_when.note: Source explicitly notes that expert intuition in well-trained stable domains is accurate — the avoid_when reflects this with the expert-domain carve-out.

input_type.note: Compact label reviewed normalization of the source's framing of System 1 as the generator of fast impressions and pattern-matched outputs.

output_type.note: Model's operational value is as a scrutiny trigger — identifying when System 2 should be recruited rather than accepting the fast output.

reasoning_types.note: Diagnostic — identifying when bias is operating; metacognitive — reasoning about the quality of one's own fast cognitive processes.
